review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1. Good Initial Pay
2. Perks and renumerations are really good (especially medical benefits)
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
The following points are solely based on my own perspective and experiences having worked there for two years.
1) Remote Location - This is something you would expect from any PSU, majority of GAIL'S plants, project sites etc are at remote locations, even though most locations have a good township but people who are used to living in metro cities would find it difficult to adjust.
2) Job Profile - This I think is the least talked about point(ignored by majority)but it is the most significant one. Profile varies location wise, if you happen to get posted in one of their process plants than you would get a good technical exposure but in smaller stations or project/construction sites you would be doing work which will have absolutely no relevance with any engineering you have studied, it would mostly be about managing or supervising daily routine works , field/site visits, obtaining various clearances from local authorities, issuing crop compensation to land owners, attending public hearings/panchnamas, overall your technical exposure would be none.
3) Growth is limited : Salary wise(yearly only 3% increment), work wise and learning opportunities wise it's very limited.
4) Work -life balance : This is a myth, its a common notion that there is no work pressure at PSU , aaram ki zindagi hain, baiithe baithe paisa kamaoge but reality is completely different , if you happen to get posted at pipeline project/construction sites or in shift duty for operation department than there would be no work life balance.
